name: Black and Blinding
description: The Duergar machines have driven the Quartz-Mother from her nest. Find a way to silence the Deepmark — or put her down.
notes: Worked example. Duergar parley → silence ritual / hunt / relocate. Ties to The Silence Beneath (#5) and The Smith Who Sold the Sun (#11).

stages.title: Parley with the miners
stages.objective: Speak with the Duergar crew gnawing at the crystal seams.

stages.title: Seek the captain
stages.objective: Find Kopmter Coffer and put the hunt to him.

name: The Tide That Forgets
description: Elthiad's tides are rising wrong — drowning farmland — and the elders say the old water-wards have gone silent.
notes: Knot: (a) restore the ward / (b) repurpose it to drain a path to a deeper ruin / (c) let the water reclaim the town / (d) rebuild it as a foreign machine. Gated: only Maren gets the 'the water remembers her blood' kinship beat. Spun out: Tideward Glyph (reusable ruin key), Breathwater Draught (underwater breathing). Recurring NPC: Sela Marsh-Voice.

name: The Phoenix Pilgrims
description: A procession of zealots marches on burning Bouquet to 'rekindle the rite', dragging captives for kindling. They believe the heir is dead.
notes: Knot: (a) stop by force / (b) infiltrate & sway / (c) shadow them to a safe route through the heat / (d) free only the captives. Gated: only Florian can attempt the 'the crown commands you' submission ending. Spun out: Emberward Charm (fire resist), Cold Crystal (push into the burn). Recurring NPC: Cantor Hale.

name: The Long End
description: The Strifts offer a 'fair' trade for a Crystalman artifact-half — but their deals always take the long end.
notes: Knot: (a) trade and find the hidden cost later / (b) steal it / (c) expose their scam / (d) force a genuinely fair deal (needs leverage you go earn). Gated: Vorrin-only 'haggle to a true-fair price'. Spun out: Truth-Weight Scale (detect deceit), Crystalman Artifact Half (Drevok). Recurring NPC: Quartermaster Ix.

name: The Mothers' Hollow
description: Mothers and children vanish from a frontier hamlet by night; the only trace is chalk-smell and a humming in the walls. Pale Listeners are nesting.
notes: Knot: (a) burn the nest (loud) / (b) lure & trap them by sound (their weakness) / (c) wall up and evacuate / (d) leash a captured Listener to find the taken. Gated: Maren-only 'they're communicating' investigation. Spun out: Resonance Bell / Echo Lure, Chalk-Glyph Codex. Recurring NPC: Goodwife Orla.

name: The Silence Beneath
description: A monastery's bells won't stop ringing (a compulsion curse), and the noise has woken something in the crypt. The only way down is through unbearable sound.
notes: Origin of the Silence Ritual. Knot: (a) learn/cast a Silence Ritual to pass quietly / (b) smash the bells (enrages the monks) / (c) endure and fight through the din. Gated: Ysolde-only 'absolve the bound monks'. Spun out: Ritual of Silence / Veil of Quiet (flagship reusable spell), Wax-Sealed Ears. Recurring NPC: Abbot Veheld.

name: Salt and Ash
description: A caravan is stranded on the seam between the freezing salt flats and the burning Cinder Bloom; a storm shuts both routes.
notes: Showcases warmth/freezing. Knot: (a) lead them through the cold / (b) through the heat / (c) split the train / (d) shelter and wait out attrition. Gated: Drevok-only 'engineer a climate rig'. Spun out: Travelling Brazier / Warmstone, Stormglass; reuses Emberward Charm (#2). Recurring NPC: Caravan-Master Bren.

name: The Clockwork Grief
description: A gnome enclave's great clock-shrine has stopped; with it, the memory-automata holding their ancestors' recorded minds are winding down.
notes: Knot: (a) repair the clock / (b) let it stop / (c) transplant the memories into Crystalman storage / (d) keep one mind, lose the rest. Gated: Drevok-only 'rebuild it better than they could'. Spun out: Memory-Cog / Soul-Spindle, seed for a Clockwork Familiar. Recurring NPC: Tik the Lesser.

name: The Drowned Choir
description: A chart leads to a sunken Elthiad outpost — claimed by a feral amphibian sect that worships the drowned dead and suffers no outsiders.
notes: Feeds Maren's Sunken Bastion. Knot: (a) win their trust / (b) take by force / (c) bargain (recover a relic for passage) / (d) desecrate quietly and slip past. Gated: only Maren (amphibian) gets the kinship ending; everyone else must fight or bargain. Spun out: reuse Breathwater + Tideward Glyph (#1), Drowned-Tongue Phrasebook. Recurring NPC: Deepmother Yssa.

name: A Crown of Thorns and Flowers
description: A surviving Bouquet noble holds the dead court 'until the heir returns' — and won't believe Florian is real.
notes: Florian's win-condition core. Knot: (a) prove his identity / (b) depose the regent by force / (c) abdicate and let the steward keep the throne / (d) share rule. Gated: only Florian can claim the throne. Spun out: Crown of Flowers (McGuffin), Ashbloom Seed. Recurring NPC: Steward Maliere.

name: The Hungering Quiet
description: A giant-insect hive has gone silent and still — not dead, waiting. The swamp folk dread a swarm-bloom.
notes: Knot: (a) exterminate the hive / (b) redirect the swarm / (c) cure what silenced the queen (a fouled spring) / (d) harvest the dormant hive and leave it crippled. Gated: Vaelen-only 'weaponise the swarm'. Spun out: Queen's Resin (pacify/enrage insects), Chitin Plating armour. Recurring NPC: Hedge-Witch Mire.

name: The Smith Who Sold the Sun
description: A legendary Duergar-forged sun-metal blade surfaces on the market — looted from a massacre, fenced alongside the dead's heirlooms.
notes: Vorrin's core; Kopmter callback. Knot: (a) buy it (funds the fence) / (b) return the heirlooms to survivors / (c) expose/rob the fence / (d) take the blade and look away. Gated: Vorrin-only 'reclaim it as Coffer work' (ties to Kopmter / Black and Blinding). Spun out: Sun-Metal Ingot, Maker's Mark Loupe. Recurring NPC: 'Honest' Pell + Kopmter Coffer.

name: The Light That Lies
description: A light-elf envoy offers Vaelen's freed people 'peace' — a treaty that is re-enslavement in fine print. The freed are split: safety vs freedom.
notes: Purest example of ending-gating. Knot: (a) reject and fight / (b) sign and subvert from within / (c) expose the buried clauses / (d) flee/scatter the people. Gated: the conciliatory 'sign in good faith' ending is CLOSED to Vaelen — the wound is too deep. Spun out: Oathglass (reveal a contract's true wording), Freedmark Brand. Recurring NPC: Envoy Caeloth.

name: The Cookfire Truce
description: Two starving warbands are dug in around the one working well in a ruin; open war and both sides starve. A shared meal could buy a truce — if no one poisons the pot.
notes: Cooking-artisanry showcase. Knot: (a) broker the feast-truce / (b) pick a side and break the siege / (c) poison one side / (d) seize the well for yourself. Gated: Florian-led 'both captains break bread' has the widest reach. Spun out: Hearthfeast Recipe / Morale Stew, Tastetongue Charm. Recurring NPC: Old Pots + the two warband captains.

name: The Buried Library
description: A collapsed Crystalman library is found beneath a bastion — its language-locked doors and trap-glyphs guarding knowledge many want, and some want gone.
notes: Archaeology showcase; feeds Maren & Drevok's Crystalman threads. Knot: (a) open it for all (dangerous) / (b) claim it for one faction / (c) reseal it / (d) loot and collapse it. Gated: Maren-only full translation (others get fragments). Spun out: Crystalman Lexicon (a reusable language key), Glyph-Trap Kit. Recurring NPC: Castellan Voss.

name: The Mistwalkers' Bargain
description: Travellers lost in the Darkmist return changed — speaking in unison, leading others back in. They offer the 'gift' of the mist's clarity. The Darkmist is recruiting.
notes: Capstone of the Crystalman/Darkmist throughline; Drevok's core. Knot: (a) break the beacon drawing people in / (b) study the mist-touched / (c) bargain with what speaks through them (a lasting cost) / (d) wall off the region and abandon the taken. Gated: Drevok-only 'pierce it with the assembled artifact' (needs the halves from #3/#7/#14). Spun out: Mistlantern, Clarity Draught. Recurring NPC: Calder, the First Mistwalker.
